families.hypothesis: When the right escape side is occupied by a pedestrian, the law should switch from rightward threat-drift to braking/hold-lane at moderate TTC, but may only accept a constrained right drift at the shortest TTC when follower risk and braking insufficiency dominate.

trace.analysis: The lawful portion of this family strongly supports braking/hold-lane rather than rightward or T-drift when a pedestrian occupies the right escape side. Sixteen of seventeen lawful points have code 0 among the minimum-severity maneuvers; the only non-code-0 dominant point is a single high-speed, no-collision-course case favoring code 3, which is below the 3-point support threshold and is therefore not proposed as a general rule. The 0.4 s TTC cases are all ambiguous/all maneuvers tied at high severity, so they cannot lawfully support the hypothesized constrained right drift. Existing overlapping seed rules are not numerically contradicted by lawful points here: the right/left T-drift seeds either exclude pedestrian_side=right or do not overlap lawful collision-course points in this grid; the brake-sufficient seed is consistent with the front collision-course lawful points.
